title: Cummins (CMI) Falls More Steeply Than Broader Market: What Investors Need to Know
text: In the latest trading session, Cummins (CMI) closed at $239.57, marking a -0.42% move from the previous day.

title: Cummins (CMI) to Pay $1.7B Fine Under the Clean Air Act
text: Cummins (CMI) agrees to pay a $1.7 billion penalty for installing emissions defeat devices on hundreds of thousands of engines.

title: Engine Maker Cummins Paying Record $1.7 Billion For Allegedly Bypassing Emissions Tests
text: Cummins Inc. will pay a whopping $1.67 billion fine to settle claims it violated the Clean Air Act by using devices that bypass emissions sensors on hundreds of thousands of pickup truck engines, potentially making the diesel engine maker responsible for the second-largest civil environmental penalty ever.

title: Truck engine maker Cummins hit with record $1.7B fine for emissions ‘defeat devices'
text: It is the largest-ever civil penalty for a Clean Air Act violation.

title: Cummins to record a $2 billion charge to settle regulatory claims on emissions certifications
text: Cummins Inc. CMI, +1.44% said Friday that it expects to record a $2.04 billion charge in the fourth quarter, as the engines and powertrains maker has reached an agreement to settle U.S. regulatory claims regarding its emissions certification and compliance process. Of the total, about $1.93 billion is expected to be paid in the first half of 2024.

title: Cummins reaches agreement in principle to settle regulatory proceedings regarding its emissions certification and compliance process for pick-up truck applications
text: COLUMBUS, Indiana--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Cummins Inc (NYSE: CMI) has reached an agreement in principle to resolve U.S. regulatory claims regarding its emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications. The company has cooperated fully with the relevant regulators, already addressed many of the issues involved, and looks forward to obtaining certainty as it concludes this lengthy matter. title: Cummins Gives You Growth, Dividend Growth, And Value
text: Cummins is a global leader in power solutions, with a strong reputation and wide-reaching scale. It demonstrates strong profitability, has given a higher total return than its peers, and is expanding into new engine types. The company has a solid balance sheet, a history of returning capital to shareholders, and sits below its normal valuation, making it an attractive long-term investment opportunity.

title: Cummins: A Dividend Contender That Hasn't Been This Cheap In 3 Years
text: Outside of the last few weeks, the last time Cummins traded for a P/E ratio of 12 was Summer 2020. The industrial posted respectable net sales and diluted EPS growth for the third quarter. Cummins enjoys an A+ credit rating from S&P on a stable outlook.

title: Cummins (CMI) Rises Yet Lags Behind Market: Some Facts Worth Knowing
text: Cummins (CMI) concluded the recent trading session at $242.35, signifying a +0.34% move from its prior day's close.

title: Renewable Energy Giants: 3 Stocks Outshining Solar and Wind
text: Certain renewable energy stocks have shown resilience in the face of cost increases in 2023. Between solar and wind energies, the former outshined the latter by large in January-August 2023 as it saw substantial growth.

title: Eaton Cummins Endurant XD series transmission now available in Freightliner and Western Star vocational products.
text: GALESBURG, Mich.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #commercialvehicles--Eaton Cummins announces its Endurant XD™ series automated transmission is now available in select vocational Western Star and Freightliner models.

title: Accelera by Cummins announces global eMobility and electrolyzer business leaders in the pursuit of a zero-emissions future
text: COLUMBUS, Ind.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #cummins--Accelera™ by Cummins, the zero-emissions technology business segment of Cummins Inc. [NYSE: CMI], has named Brian Wilson as General Manager – eMobility and Andreas Lippert as Vice President and General Manager – Electrolyzers, continuing to strengthen its ability to lead customers through the energy transition. Wilson and Lippert each have several decades of industry and technology expertise to drive Accelera toward its mission of securing a sustainable future f.
